item.title: Wall St Week Ahead-US jobs data to give economic view for war-gripped markets
item.author: EBR European Business Report
item.description: Meanwhile, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield has climbed to 4.4% from about 4% before the war started. "The equity market is also taking very careful notice" of the rise in yields, said David Bianco, Americas chief investment officer at DWS. "This affects so many things," he said, including mortgages, the debt sustainability of the U.S. government and what is a fair price-to-earnings valuation.
